notes_this_run: Mode 2 (Human Notes) processed: HNOTE-OPERATOR-EXP-254-9-STATION-COVERAGE-001 consumed by writing EXP-260. New addendum to §4.2.7 (the eclipse-geometry trilemma) gives the quantitative station-by-station kill: of the dome's 9 published eclipse stations, 0 lie within the 42-km dome-geometry penumbra radius and 0 within the 85-km band (closest Ebro at 444 km / 10.6x R_p; furthest Canary Islands at 4,084 km / 97x R_p). Constant-r orbital reading: max 1 station can be in the band at all (closest pair Coimbra-San Pablo dr=150 km > 85-km band). Inflated R_p needed for full coverage = 4,084 km, equivalent to Moon altitude ~246,000 km (97x dome's 2,534). Pre-empts time-distribution rebuttal: NN-tour 16,439 km > 15,660 km max arc at 87 km/min. New SVG eclipse-stations-vs-penumbra-band.svg embedded in EXP-260 new_files. Blocked on EXP-254 integration. Hnote pending: 0.

PHASE 3 — SINGLETON SUBSTANTIVE (ATT-2026-04-25T05:27:36Z, WIN-010 c10):
  Reason flagged ISS-1523 ("multiplicative-error claim needs proof or softening — persisting c6–c9 hole") as the priority concern. Verification:
  (a) ISS-1523 status: FIXED via EXP-258, integrated 2026-04-26T13:27:55.791Z (today).
  (b) WIN-010 detail_verdict_text (9477c) no longer contains "approximately common multiplicative error" (the unargued claim).
  (c) Now contains "sub-dominant compared to the geographic-vs-magnetic-latitude fingerprint" + a pre-registered sensitivity check with α ∈ {0.8, 1.0, 1.2} for the post-2026-08-12 analysis, including an explicit "we will say so" falsification commit if the ranking flips.
  This fix is STRONGER than the issue's minimum-acceptable softening because it (i) flags the methodological limit explicitly, (ii) commits α range in advance (preventing post-hoc tuning), (iii) makes the failure mode public. The decider's EXP-258 work is solid.
  ISS-1522 (Chapman–Sq em-dash hyphenation) and ISS-1524 (K&C page range / DOI footnote) remain OPEN but are cosmetic/low-stakes — outside the scope of this attention item's primary flagged concern.

USER-PREFERENCE DIRECTIVE ("Think about the other side too"): Considered the strongest defender objection — that {0.8, 1.0, 1.2} is too narrow if true Sq-conductivity nonlinearity sits far from α=1 (e.g., α=0.5 or 1.5). Accepted as a known limit and noted that the WIN itself acknowledges this ("the magnitude of any nonlinear-coverage correction is not derived in this WIN"). The pre-registration constraint matters more than range width — selecting α to fit results is precluded regardless of bracket width. Counter-argument considered and accepted; resolution stands.
